answer: Argon2id with parameters: 64MB memory, 3 iterations, 4 parallelism
Refresh token expiration: 7 days with rotate-on-use policy
Notification Service runs on port 9200
Currently running PostgreSQL 15.4, upgraded from 14.9
API Gateway throttle limits: 5000 burst, 2000 steady state requests per second
Argon2id chosen over bcrypt for side-channel resistance
Drizzle chosen over Prisma for zero runtime dependencies and SQL-like TypeScript syntax
Both services validate JWT tokens via JWKS endpoint accessed through the Lambda authorizer which injects claims into requests
Rate limiting at ALB layer provides DDoS protection before requests reach containers, more efficient than application-level rate limiting

Gateway validates schema, Lambda authorizer validates JWT via JWKS and injects claims, VPCLink routes to ALB (1000 req/min/IP rate limit), then Auth Service verifies role via RBAC middleware before Portfolio Service processes rebalance request

If JWKS endpoint goes down, all downstream auth validation fails for all services. Private key remains local for continued token issuance. LRU cache provides grace period: 1000 entries with 5-minute TTL
Real-time portfolio alerting depends on both Notify Service and Gateway WebSocket API. Status: Planned for Q3
Yes, UTC+14 token expiry bug fixed by using Unix timestamps instead of Date objects for timezone-aware expiry calculations

Implement webhook registration at POST /api/v1/notifications/webhooks/register endpoint with exponential backoff retry policy (5 attempts over 24 hours) for auth events like login, MFA setup, and role changes
Maximum simultaneous connections: 5 Auth Service instances × 20 connections per instance = 100 total pool connections

Connection pool of 20/instance limits concurrent database operations during tax-lot processing. Celery memory is unbounded during large portfolio recalc tasks, which is a separate issue being investigated
No, Fargate Spot cannot be used for Auth Service because of interrupt risk. Spot is only permitted for batch jobs like portfolio recalculation and digest generation, not always-on services

Multi-service architecture doctrine prohibits in-memory session cache to Auth Service because distributed services cannot share memory; state must be externalized (JWT tokens, Redis) to maintain consistency across service instances
30-day backup retention exceeds RPO requirement of 1 hour and supports RTO of 4 hours by enabling recovery of data up to 30 days in the past, with cross-region replication to us-west-2
Connection string changed to sslmode=verify-full with global-bundle.pem certificate validation after RDS upgrade from 14.9 to 15.4
Tax_lots table columns: id, account_id, security_id, acquisition_date, cost_basis, quantity, adjusted, created_at

MFA affects login flow by requiring additional POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/setup step with QR code generation for TOTP setup. If MFA enabled, login requires TOTP verification and 10 single-use recovery codes are generated for backup access
10 recovery codes are generated and each is single-use and permanently consumed upon use, cannot be reused
Webhook retry policy: exponential backoff with maximum 5 attempts over 24 hours

Security posture for credentials: At rest - AES-256-GCM encryption with per-tenant keys, passwords hashed with Argon2id (64MB/3iter/4par). In transit - mTLS via Envoy service mesh, database connections use sslmode=verify-full with global-bundle.pem. Secrets rotated every 90 days

Full login lifecycle with MFA: Gateway validates schema → Lambda validates JWT via JWKS → VPCLink routes to ALB (1000 req/min/IP limit) → Auth Service handlePostLogin → Argon2id password verify → if MFA enabled, require TOTP token validation and verify recovery code status → issue RS256 access token (15min expiry) + refresh token (7day rotate-on-use) → return to client

New Billing Service in Rust must follow: handle{Verb}{Resource} naming conventions (e.g. handleGetBillingReport), use RS256 JWT verification via JWKS, implement RFC7807 Problem Details error responses with traceId extension, use real PostgreSQL via testcontainers in tests, structured JSON logging, AsyncLocalStorage for request ID tracking, require Claude Code review before merge, deploy via GitHub Actions CI/CD with blue-green deployment

1500 req/min from single IP: ALB enforces 1000 req/min per IP rate limit → 500 excess requests blocked → Lambda authorizer validates remaining requests via JWKS → Auth Service pool limit (20 connections) may queue requests if traffic spikes → service returns 429 Too Many Requests after rate limit breach

Systems depending on JWT signing key: Auth Service (issues tokens with private key), Lambda authorizer (validates via public key in JWKS), all downstream services (Portfolio, Notification) receive validated claims injected by Lambda, AppMesh/Envoy (mTLS certificate management), token rotation mechanism, JWKS endpoint (/.well-known/jwks.json) serving public key

Deployment pipeline: Developer commits to GitHub → GitHub Actions triggers (lint → typecheck → test → build) → build Docker image → push to ECR → Claude Code review required before merge to main → approval triggers ECS deployment → blue-green ALB target switch for zero-downtime deployment → canary monitoring of new version → rollback capability if needed
